title: Remove theme toggle and enforce always-on dark styling

requirements.summary: Remove the theme toggle control from the header so users cannot switch themes in-app.
acceptanceCriteria: The header no longer renders a theme toggle button/icon.
No UI control remains that toggles between light and dark modes.
The app builds successfully with no unused/failed imports related to the removed toggle.

file_operations.description: Remove the ThemeToggle import and stop rendering the ThemeToggle in the header actions area.
Delete the ThemeToggle component to ensure no theme toggle UI can be reintroduced via leftover code.

requirements.summary: Force dark styling on every load (ignoring system preference and any stored theme) using existing Tailwind class-based dark variables, with no light-theme flash.
acceptanceCriteria: On first load (fresh browser profile), the app renders in dark theme.
If localStorage contains theme='light' from a previous version/session, the app still renders in dark theme on load.
There is no flash of light theme before dark theme is applied (no visible FOUC).

file_operations.description: Update the pre-React theme bootstrapping so the document is always rendered in dark mode (ensure the 'dark' class is applied immediately on load) and ignore/neutralize any previously stored theme value to prevent a light-theme flash.

requirements.summary: Remove client-side theme persistence and runtime toggling logic now that the app is always dark.
acceptanceCriteria: No code path attempts to toggle the document 'dark' class at runtime based on a stored theme value.
Theme-related code is either deleted or left unused-free (no ESLint/TypeScript errors and no dead imports in the app entry/layout components).

file_operations.description: Delete the theme state/persistence hook that reads/writes localStorage and toggles the document 'dark' class at runtime.
Retire any remaining theme persistence logic in the early theme script (stop reading localStorage/system preference for theme selection) so no runtime path can re-enable light mode.
